user_input: I am likely experiencing a severe shoulder injury, possibly a rotator cuff tear or ligament damage. The pain started about two weeks ago after lifting a heavy object. It is located in the front and outer part of my shoulder and sometimes spreads down my arm. The pain is very intense, around 9 out of 10, and feels sharp during movement with a deep throbbing ache at rest, along with weakness and tingling sensations. This has been persistent for the past two weeks without relief. I have no significant previous history of shoulder injuries, but I occasionally had mild strain due to physical activity. I am finding it very difficult to lift my arm, raise it above shoulder level, or move it normally. I do not have any major medical conditions, and my overall health has been normal. However, I have noticed some concerning red flags such as a large painful swelling (around 8–9 inches), spreading sensation, and numbness with tingling in my arm. I have tried anti-inflammatory medications, rest, and even consulted a doctor, but there has been no significant improvement. Based on these symptoms, I suspect it could be a rotator cuff tear or ligament injury.
